title: Tax Audit u/s 44AB Chartered Accountant | Virtual Auditor
description: Tax audit under Section 44AB by expert CA. Form 3CA/3CB/3CD preparation. Business and professional audit. Offices in Chennai, Bangalore, Mumbai.
bodyText:
Looking for a chartered accountant for tax audit under Section 44AB? Virtual Auditor provides comprehensive tax audit services across India, led by CA V. Viswanathan — Fellow Chartered Accountant (FCA). We handle Form 3CA/3CB and Form 3CD preparation with thorough compliance checks for businesses and professionals.

What is Tax Audit u/s 44AB Chartered Accountant | Virtual Auditor?

  HEADING2: What We Deliver
  Tax audit under Section 44AB — complete audit of books of accounts for businesses (turnover > Rs 1 crore) and professionals (gross receipts > Rs 50 lakhs). Form 3CA (for entities already subject to statutory audit) or Form 3CB (for other entities) — audit report with observations and qualifications. Form 3CD — statement of particulars covering 44 detailed clauses including TDS compliance, GST reconciliation, depreciation computation, Section 40(a)(ia) disallowances, and ICDS adjustments. MAT/AMT computation under Section 115JB/115JC. Clause-by-clause compliance verification with detailed working papers. Tax audit report e-filing through the income tax portal before the due date (30 September / 31 October).
  HEADING2: Why Choose Virtual Auditor for Tax Audit
  Tax audit under Section 44AB is not a routine compliance — it requires deep understanding of income tax provisions, ICDS (Income Computation and Disclosure Standards), GST reconciliation (Clause 44), TDS compliance (Clause 34), and related party transactions. CA V. Viswanathan brings practitioner-level expertise across all Form 3CD clauses. Common audit adjustments that less experienced CAs miss: ICDS adjustments for revenue recognition, construction contracts, and inventory valuation; Section 43B disallowances for outstanding statutory dues; and Section 40(a)(ia) disallowances for TDS non-compliance. Our tax audit identifies these issues before assessment, preventing demand notices.
  HEADING2: Our Process
  Step 1: Engagement and planning — understand business, assess risk areas, design audit programme. Step 2: Books of accounts verification — completeness, accuracy, and compliance with accounting standards. Step 3: Clause-by-clause Form 3CD preparation with detailed workings. Step 4: TDS compliance review (Clause 34) with default identification. Step 5: GST turnover reconciliation (Clause 44) with books and returns. Step 6: Draft report discussion with management. Step 7: Final report preparation and e-filing before due date.
  HEADING2: Get Started Today
  Need tax audit under Section 44AB? Contact Virtual Auditor:
  Call/WhatsApp: +91-99622 60333 (Chennai) | +91-95139 39333 (Bangalore) | +91-77000 89597 (Mumbai)
  Email: support@virtualauditor.in
  Offices: Chennai (Spencer Plaza) | Bangalore (MG Road) | Mumbai (Goregaon West)

faqs.q: Who needs tax audit under Section 44AB?
faqs.a: Businesses with turnover exceeding Rs 1 crore (Rs 10 crores if cash receipts and payments are within 5% of total). Professionals with gross receipts exceeding Rs 50 lakhs. Also applicable when presumptive taxation under Section 44AD/44ADA is opted but income is below the prescribed percentage.

faqs.q: What is the due date for tax audit report?
faqs.a: 30 September of the assessment year (e.g., 30 September 2026 for FY 2025-26). If transfer pricing provisions apply, the due date is 31 October. Late filing of tax audit report attracts penalty of 0.5% of turnover or Rs 1.5 lakhs, whichever is lower, under Section 271B.

faqs.q: Can the same CA do both statutory audit and tax audit?
faqs.a: For companies, the statutory auditor typically also does the tax audit using Form 3CA. For non-corporate entities, any practising CA can be appointed as tax auditor. There is no restriction on the same CA performing both audits.
